violations: CRITICAL: Plan follows outdated PLAN.md structure from separate analysis/pseudocode/implementation phases instead of the required stub→TDD→impl→verification pattern
Phases are numbered 01-12 instead of following the required stub→TDD→impl→verification cycle
Line-numbered pseudocode is provided in separate files but not properly referenced in implementation phases
Missing property-based testing requirements (30% minimum specified in PLAN.md)
Missing mutation testing requirements (80% minimum specified in PLAN.md)
Plan creates new test files instead of updating existing ones where appropriate
Phases do not follow the required self-contained pattern - they reference external analysis files
Some phases claim to 'DELETE' files instead of updating them (violates 'All files are UPDATED not created as new versions')
Plan structure suggests old methodology rather than current PLAN.md requirements
user_access_points: CLI: Any command using `/provider openai /model gpt-5` or `/model o3`
Save/Load: `/chat save` and `/chat load` preserve metadata automatically
History: Conversation history maintains response IDs in metadata
old_code_to_remove: Hardcoded undefined conversation tracking in OpenAIProvider
All IMessage type imports throughout codebase - Replace with Content[]

detailed_analysis.integration_strength: STRONG - Plan clearly identifies specific existing files that will use the feature and shows how users access it through CLI commands
detailed_analysis.isolation_risk: LOW - Feature integrates with existing sessionId flow from geminiChat and ContentGenerator
major_issues: Plan follows outdated methodology with numbered phases 01-12 instead of required stub→TDD→impl→verification pattern
Missing critical testing requirements (property-based and mutation testing)
Phases reference external analysis files instead of being self-contained
Some violation of 'update don't create' principle with file deletion approach
positive_aspects: Excellent integration analysis - identifies specific call chains and user access points
Clear understanding of existing architecture and integration points
Detailed specification of exact files to modify and old code to remove
Good use of line-numbered pseudocode (though not properly integrated with phases)
Feature does NOT build in isolation - integrates with existing sessionId flow

recommendation: REJECT - While the integration plan is excellent, the plan violates multiple core PLAN.md requirements around phase structure, testing requirements, and self-contained phases. The plan needs to be restructured to follow the stub→TDD→impl→verification pattern with proper mutation and property-based testing.
